message:
perf(inference): cache Beta/Gamma prior constants in compute_posterior

The posterior computation recalculates log_beta(alpha, beta) and
log_gamma(shape) normalization constants on every call to
compute_posterior, even though the prior parameters rarely change
between invocations.  For the hot path (process classification),
this dominated CPU time in profiling.

Introduces a PriorsHotPathCache that pre-computes CachedBetaPrior
and CachedGammaPrior constants per class (useful, useful_bad,
abandoned, zombie), keyed by the bit-exact representation of all
prior parameters.  The cache is stored in a global OnceLock<Mutex<
HashMap>> so it persists across calls without requiring callers to
thread state through.

Changes to public API:
  - log_lik_cpu now accepts an optional &CachedBetaPrior parameter
  - log_lik_runtime now accepts an optional &CachedGammaPrior
  - Both fall back to the uncached path when None is passed

New types (all internal):
  - PriorsHotPathCache: per-class cached normalization constants
  - PriorsHotPathKey / ClassHotPathKey: bit-exact f64 hash keys
  - ClassHotPathCache: holds optional CachedBetaPrior + CachedGammaPrior

Testing:
  - cached_cpu_fraction_matches_uncached: verifies cached and
    uncached Beta PDF agree within 1e-12 for useful and abandoned
  - cached_runtime_matches_uncached: same for Gamma PDF across
    useful and zombie classes
  - All existing log_lik_runtime tests updated to pass None
    (uncached path preserved)

Also closes beads issue bd-98q8 (profile-driven optimization pass
on deterministic hot path).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
